item.title: New Record for me. Completing the walk in 26 seconds.
item.description: Working on walking and setting a new record. Was able to finish the walk in 26 seconds. Really proud of my progress. It has been tough lately. But I will keep working hard. I hope everyone has a great day.

item.title: Greta Thunberg speaks to press after being released from illegal detainment
item.description: "I could talk for a very, very long time about our mistreatment and abuses in our imprisonment. Trust me, but that is not the story." Staying on point even after all the abuse https://www.theguardian.com/world/2025/oct/04/greta-thunberg-israel-gaza-sweden The environmental campaigner Greta Thunberg has told Swedish officials she is being subjected to harsh treatment in Israeli custody after her detention and removal from a flotilla carrying aid to Gaza, according to correspondence seen by the Guardian. According to the correspondence, Israeli forces are also reported by another detainee to have taken photographs where Thunberg was allegedly forced to hold flags. The identity of the flags is unknown. In an email sent by the Swedish foreign ministry to people close to Thunberg, and seen by the Guardian, an official who has visited the activist in prison said she claimed she was detained in a cell infested with bedbugs, with too little food and water. “The embassy has been able to meet with Greta,” reads the email. “She informed of dehydration. She has received insufficient amounts of both water and food. She also stated that she had developed rashes which she suspects were caused by bedbugs. She spoke of harsh treatment and said she had been sitting for long periods on hard surfaces.” “Another detainee reportedly told another embassy that they had seen her [Thunberg] being forced to hold flags while pictures were taken. She wondered whether images of her had been distributed,” the Swedish ministry’s official added. The allegation was corroborated by at least two other members of the flotilla who had been detained by Israeli forces and released on Saturday. “They dragged little Greta [Thunberg] by her hair before our eyes, beat her, and forced her to kiss the Israeli flag. They did everything imaginable to her, as a warning to others,” the Turkish activist Ersin Çelik, a participant in the Sumud flotilla, told Anadolu news agency.
item.dc:creator: LaughingMan2Gig

item.title: Portland, OR police, repost

item.dc:creator: OregonComputerGuy

item.title: It was not an easy job but it's all I've ever known
item.description: Don't get me wrong, the world changes and you need to adapt, but this was faster than I had ever imagine. I work, or should say worked, in visual effects. For 15 years I spent anywhere from 40 to 90 hours a week giving my best to make movies look as good as possible. For a long time I thought despite the burnouts, insane hours, forever shrinking budgets, moving across the world to find work, I would make it work because I love what I do... But COVID arrived. First layoff because shootings stopped, 6 months surviving thanks to my partner. Then the Writer Guild strike followed by the Actor strike. Another 4 months surviving on my savings. Then the government shut down the tax incentives that made us competitive. Another 3 months getting by on what I saved since the last layoff. To help us out, a wave of "no VFX was used in the making of xxxx" started on movies with hundreds of heavy VFX shots, making our work invisible, with directors borderline ashame to put our names in the credit. And now AI trying to take over the entire industry. In 2019, I was going to work with hundreds of my peers, sharing our passion and ideas until late at night often 6 days a week. Today contracts are for a few weeks at best, cameras closed during meetings, I barely know the people I work with and there's usually not more than 8 to 10 of them. Once a show is delivered, another bunch replace them. Each show I see the same struggle in everyone's life. Some have to leave the country because their work visa expired. Some struggle to pay rent and are returning home, often in a different province, if not a different country. A lot of artist I worked with are now electrician, plumbers, IT, programmers, ... And with each of them leaving, it's decades of expertise gone forever. I'm starting university soon to become a psychologist myself, after realizing that what I loved the most in my job was the people, and they are now gone. I've held on to the hope that somehow, at some point, things might get better, but I've once again been laid off, and I don't know if this time it will be 3, 4, 6 months or just permanent. LinkedIn, the hell where 95% of the job market for my field happens, is dead silent. Just AI bros sharing about the latest AI model and more AI bros dreaming of how anyone can make a movie with prompts. It makes job hunting an even worse experience as I scroll through posts hoping to see a role and instead, keep seeing more and more people abandoning VFX and random tech bros redoing scenes from their favorite movies on any random AI generator. When a car maker closes a factory, it's thousands of people losing their jobs, journalists report on it. During the Hollywood strikes the media coverage was intense. The latest story of "Tilly Norwood" seems to be everywhere. All while thousands of VFX artists like me are silently left to disappear without much coverage. It's like we were never here. Sorry for the long post, I feel like I'm yelling into the void.
item.dc:creator: logkyles

item.title: Judge Who Denied A DOJ Request For Voters Records, House Burned Down After An Explosion – SLED Is Investigating As Arson
item.description:
Liberal South Carolina Judge Diane Goodstein's home is burning to the ground after an explosion. SLED is investigating as arson and refer attack as Judge Goodstein had been receiving multiple threats. Her husband, a former State Senator was air-lifted to the hospital.
While Steven Miller rails about Judges.
SOUTH CAROLINA... "Judge Goodstein was walking on the beach when the fire started. Her husband, Arnie, was in the house with children and perhaps grandchildren. The family had to escape by jumping from a window or balcony..."

item.title: Just going to leave this here ......
item.description: Nazism triumphed in Germany during the high era of Jim Crow laws in the United States. Did the American regime of racial oppression in any way inspire the Nazis? The unsettling answer is yes. In Hitler’s American Model, James Whitman presents a detailed investigation of the American impact on the notorious Nuremberg Laws, the centerpiece anti-Jewish legislation of the Nazi regime. Contrary to those who have insisted that there was no meaningful connection between American and German racial repression, Whitman demonstrates that the Nazis took a real, sustained, significant, and revealing interest in American race policies. As Whitman shows, the Nuremberg Laws were crafted in an atmosphere of considerable attention to the precedents American race laws had to offer. German praise for American practices, already found in Hitler’s Mein Kampf, was continuous throughout the early 1930s, and the most radical Nazi lawyers were eager advocates of the use of American models. But while Jim Crow segregation was one aspect of American law that appealed to Nazi radicals, it was not the most consequential one. Rather, both American citizenship and antimiscegenation laws proved directly relevant to the two principal Nuremberg Laws—the Citizenship Law and the Blood Law. Whitman looks at the ultimate, ugly irony that when Nazis rejected American practices, it was sometimes not because they found them too enlightened, but too harsh.
item.dc:creator: Xalten2

item.title: GovBrief Today #248
item.description: ☢️ Trump dropped the façade today and ordered troops into Chicago and Portland. A federal judge has blocked any Oregon deployment for now. Illinois Governor JB Pritzker is calling this Trump's Invasion, and it's hard not to see it as anything but a pretext. The ACLU is offering free training on Zoom on Monday night at 8pm ET. You'll learn about what rights you have while protesting and how to do so safely. That will get you set for the No Kings day of mass actions in hundreds of cities nationwide. There are soldiers on the ground in four cities and he's trying to get to a fifth if he can override the judge keeping them out of Portland. Make sure you know what your rights are. Don't leave truth to the algorithm. Our free and private nightly email arms you with images, data, and quotes so you can see it all for yourself. Add your email at govbrief dot today or on Substack at Gov Brief Today. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com [1] https://go.govbrief.today/dc-molotov-arrest-cathedral [2] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-soybean-aid [3] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-guard-deployment-blocked [4] https://go.govbrief.today/national-guard-chicago-deployment [5] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-maritime-murders-kindness [6] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-sailor-back-pay [7] https://go.govbrief.today/disability-payments-age-exemptions [8] https://go.govbrief.today/free-no-kings-training
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.title: Day 1 of Making my Sauron Helmet from LOTR - YuzuPyon
item.description:
Source : https://youtu.be/pq6cIOIssgY Hello everyone, finally posting the daily vlog series of making my Sauron cosplay project! Starting with the base helmet on which I will add the details later :) I will be posting slightly longer form videos daily on my Youtube Channel to show you the process a little bit more in-depth if you're interested to follow my journey check out here : https://youtu.be/pq6cIOIssgY I wish you all a great day, thank you for all the support on this project :) Have a good day, Yuzu
Source : https://youtu.be/pq6cIOIssgY Hello everyone, finally posting the daily vlog series of making my Sauron cosplay project! Starting with the base helmet on which I will add the details later :) I will be posting slightly longer form videos daily on my Youtube Channel to show you the process a little bit more in-depth if you're interested to follow my journey check out here : https://youtu.be/pq6cIOIssgY I wish you all a great day, thank you for all the support on this project :) Have a good day, Yuzu

item.title: BOROWITZ: The texts were the work of someone with at least a seventh-grade education.”
item.description: WASHINGTON (The Borowitz Report)—A string of correctly spelled texts supposedly written by Donald J. Trump during an interview with CNN has fueled fresh speculation that he has died, experts confirmed on Monday. According to Davis Logsdon, the nation’s foremost authenticator of Trump’s digital communication, “The texts, notable not only for their correct spelling but for their proper grammar, were the work of someone with at least a seventh-grade education.” “It’s impossible to identify the author of these texts, but it was someone capable of putting words together to form complete sentences,” he added. “That rules out most of his Cabinet.” In the most troubling sign, Logsdon said, one of the texts asked if the Nobel Peace Prize could be awarded posthumously—and featured a correct spelling of the word “posthumously.”
item.dc:creator: JasonM1

item.title: Have some memes! Part Twenty-two

Twin Peaks
Replicating a meal?
My favorite poem
Tis the season!
It meeeee
Hehehe
And yet, I romanced Garrus
Berserk
Me with my best friend
Another shitty edit and true story
Get your hands off me!
Inb4 someone says, Twin Peaks came out in the 90s
Berserk
It really is my favorite meme lol
A cafe sign I saw!
She likes Chappell
JoJo's Bizarre Adventure
My absolute favorite, @danat101 sent me this
Love me some Mass Effect
Mm juicy bits
Accurate
Don't do this, though
*explodes*
Correct!
We're in the music era of my meme folder
Another one that makes me go "hehehe"
What a bop!
Mhm, but also glad they do
Rude
It's true
I can't argue with that
Can you guess one of my favorite bands?
It me
As one does
I didn't crop the signature, but it's Jake Likes Onions
Working customer service be like
More Twin Peaks, I should've just made an album
Me at home
I don't think anyone finds this as amusing as me, sorry!
>.>
Another accurate portrayal of me
Hell yes
Duly noted
No, no you will not
You goober
What a wild episode
Keep this in mind, okay?
